key_insight: The paper replaces the usual ODE view of linear TD(0) with an SDE approximation that captures both mean contraction and stochastic error-floor effects under Markovian noise.
engineering_relevance: This gives a more accurate tool for predicting constant-stepsize TD performance in reinforcement learning systems where sampling data are temporally correlated.
method: Derive a diffusion approximation for linear TD(0) with Markovian sampling noise.
Separate projected Bellman operator contraction from noise-driven fluctuations.
Link the steady-state error floor to long-run covariance and contraction geometry.
evidence_signal: The abstract presents a theoretical model-based result rather than an empirical benchmark, using the SDE approximation to explain the asymptotic error floor.
watch_item: The approximation is tailored to linear features and Markovian noise, so care is needed when extending it to nonlinear function approximation or nonstationary data.
paper_title: A Diffusion Approximation for Temporal-Difference Learning with Linear Features under Markovian Noise
